news.summary: President Donald Trump is taking a risky approach by utilizing tariffs as a means to fund potential future tax cuts. While the proposed import duties are expected to bring in significant tax revenue, they could also lead to slower economic growth and increased financial burden on consumers. A report by the Tax Foundation analyzed the potential impact of Trump's tariff plan, which was introduced on April 2. It predicted that these tariffs could result in the largest tax increase since 1982 and cause a 0.7% decrease in the gross domestic product over the next ten years, without factoring in possible retaliation from other countries. The new tariff proposal aims to raise the average tariff rate on all imports from 2.5% to 16.5% by 2025, a level not seen since 1937 during the Great Depression. The Tax Foundation estimates that this plan, in conjunction with previous tariff escalations, could generate $2.9 trillion.
news.headline: Trump's tariff strategy could lead to biggest tax hike since 1982, GDP shrinking by 0.7% over next decade, raising average tariff rate from 2.5% to 16.5% by 2025.

news.summary: In a significant policy shift, President Donald Trump revealed plans to boost U.S. coal production aiming to meet the nation's increasing energy needs. This move starkly contrasts with the environmentally-focused agendas of the Biden and Obama administrations. On another front, fears of a global economic slowdown surged as Wall Street faced a steep decline due to escalating tariff tensions with China. Trump's focus is on reviving coal production and getting miners back to work by streamlining mining permits, cutting environmental regulations, and expanding coal leases on federal lands. Trump emphasized coal as the most reliable and potent energy form on Earth. U.S. Energy Information Administration data shows coal production in 2023 at 578 million short tons, a considerable decrease from the 1.17 billion short tons previously produced.
news.headline: Trump announces pro-coal policies amid stock market plunge and trade tensions with China. Focus on reviving coal production to meet energy demands. Trump vows to reopen plants, create jobs, slash regulations. US coal production in 2023 at 578 million short tons, half of previous year.

news.summary: Reitmans (Canada) Limited reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and year ended February 1, 2025. Net revenues decreased to $773.8 million for the year and $204.8 million for the quarter when excluding the additional week in the prior year. Comparable sales decreased slightly for the year and were flat for the quarter. Gross profit % increased to 56.2% for the year. Adjusted EBITDA decreased to $25.4 million for the year and was a loss of $2.6 million for the quarter. Net earnings decreased to $12.1 million for the year and was a loss of $4.2 million for the quarter. The company aims to grow to $1 billion in annual net revenue with Adjusted EBITDA to reach $60-70 million by fiscal 2030. Reitmans operates 390 stores under three banners. The company ended the year with a strong balance sheet and made strategic decisions to optimize operations. The Company is hosting a conference call to discuss its financial results. The Company granted an aggregate of 25,000 options to a member of management. Non-GAAP financial measures were used, including Adjusted EBITDA and working capital, to provide further insight into the Company's financial performance. Management believes Adjusted EBITDA is an important indicator of liquidity.
news.headline: Reitmans (Canada) plans to hit $1 billion in annual net revenues by 2030. Q4 net revenues decreased 2.9% to $204.8 million. Gross profit % was up to 56.2% for the year. Adjusted EBITDA decreased to $25.4 million for the year. Net earnings were a loss of $4.2 million for the quarter. Company details its new five-year growth strategy. For more, visit Reitmans' site. Contact VP, Corporate Communications at (514) 384-1140 ext 23737.
EU leaders plan July trip to Beijing for summit with Xi, South China Morning Post

news.summary: Keysight Technologies, Inc. revealed the pricing of a public offering of senior unsecured fixed rate notes in the sum of $750 million. These notes, set to mature in 2030, will carry an annual interest rate of 5.350 percent. The offering is anticipated to conclude on April 17, 2025, pending standard closing conditions. Keysight plans to utilize the net proceeds for undisclosed purposes.
news.headline: Keysight Technologies prices $750M senior unsecured notes at 5.350% interest, to mature in 2030. Offering to close on April 17, 2025.

news.summary: In a recent report, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has increased by 2.3% compared to last year, reaching a current rate of 7.6%. This rise in unemployment is primarily attributed to the ongoing economic challenges and uncertainties facing various industries. The job market has seen a decline of 1.5 million available jobs, with the total number of unemployed individuals now standing at 3.9 million. Experts believe that immediate measures need to be taken to address this growing concern and to stimulate job creation in order to stabilize the economy.
news.headline: Nasdaq seeks to list VanEck Avalanche ETF with a rule change filing.

news.summary: XENICAL continues to be a widely known choice for managing obesity due to its ability to lead to substantial weight loss and metabolic improvements by preventing the absorption of fats through a distinctive mechanism. Despite these advantages, its frequent occurrence of gastrointestinal side effects and moderate effectiveness in comparison to more recent therapies restrict its popularity.
news.headline: XENICAL is a leading obesity management option, but its effectiveness is limited due to gastrointestinal side effects & modest efficacy.
Brazil to auction up to 20,000 foreign exchange swaps on April 11

news.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange HashKey received approval from Hong Kong regulators on April 10 to offer staking services, making it one of the first regulated exchanges in Hong Kong to do so. This approval allows HashKey to provide staking services for spot Ether (ETH) ETFs, potentially broadening the institutional appeal of such investments. The China Securities Regulatory Commission acknowledged the benefits of crypto staking services, emphasizing the security of blockchain networks and potential returns for investors. Hong Kong approved its first Ether and Bitcoin (BTC) ETFs in April last year, giving institutional investors access to digital assets. In comparison, the United States Securities and Exchange Commission allowed spot Ether ETFs but not with staking strategies included. Many US investors seek the inclusion of staking to attract institutional investors. Approval for US Ether ETFs with staking services could come as early as May as per Bloomberg analyst James Seyffart. This development showcases the growing importance of staking in the cryptocurrency market.
news.headline: HashKey, a Hong Kong crypto exchange, approved for staking services; broadening appeal for ETH ETFs. China and HK regulators recognize benefits of staking. US investors eyeing staking inclusion in Ether ETFs post SEC green-light for spot Ether ETFs last year. HK leading in digital assets and staking services. Source: James Seyffart, Bloomberg analyst. Approval timeline by May.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, P. Peter Pascali, the President and CEO of PyroGenesis Inc., a high-tech company specializing in plasma atomized metal powders, environmentally friendly plasma waste-to-energy systems, and clean plasma torch products, has announced the filing of an updated early warning report. The report pertains to his holdings in PyroGenesis and a planned disposition of up to 850,000 common shares through the Toronto Stock Exchange. This transaction is set to be conducted by the Trust under an automatic securities disposition plan. Mr. Pascali has also submitted a notice of intention to distribute securities, which is accessible on PyroGenesis's SEDAR+ profile at www.sedarplus.ca.
news.headline: P. Peter Pascali to dispose up to 850,000 shares of PyroGenesis Inc. through Toronto Stock Exchange, as per updated report.

news.summary: In the near future, approximately four to five companies are expected to finalize agreements as part of a collective announcement. These deals are the result of the president's intensified measures against an industry that has garnered his disapproval.
news.headline: Multiple law firms nearing concessions with Trump in his crackdown, with 4 or 5 possibly agreeing to deals soon.

news.summary: In San Juan, Puerto Rico, as of April 10, 2025, it has been reported that about 90% of individuals in the population aged 65 and above are on daily medication, with many juggling between 4 to 5 prescriptions at a time. This highlights the crucial importance of medication adherence and safety in the region. One proposed solution to enhance efficiency in this area is the implementation of standardized packaging, aimed at simplifying the process for these individuals.
news.headline: Farmacias Plaza & Alivia Health introduce glow-in-the-dark medication labels in Puerto Rico to enhance patient safety. 90% of the population aged 65 or older takes daily medication, managing 4 to 5 prescriptions concurrently.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, Paranovus Entertainment Technology Limited, known as PAVS and listed as PAVS on NASDAQ, revealed they had received a Deficiency Letter from Nasdaq on April 9, 2025. This notification addresses certain regulatory compliance concerns.
news.headline: Paranovus Entertainment Technology Limited (NASDAQ: PAVS) receives Nasdaq's notification on late Form 6-K filing.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. from Rolling Meadows, Illinois, shared the successful finalization of their acquisition of Woodruff Sawyer, headquartered in San Francisco, California. This acquisition marks a strategic move by Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (NYSE: AJG), a prominent player in the global insurance brokerage and risk management sector. The completion of this deal signifies a significant step in the company's expansion and consolidation efforts within the industry.
news.headline: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. acquires Woodruff Sawyer in San Francisco. Acquisition completed on April 10, 2025.

news.summary: After experiencing significant gains on Wednesday, stocks faced a decline due to concerns over Trump's tariffs, resulting in a drop in the dollar's value. The market is anticipating two crucial inflation reports. Dow Jones Futures were impacted by the uncertainty, leading to a tumble in stocks and the dollar. Companies such as Tesla, Nvidia, and Palantir experienced a decline in their stock prices on this day.
news.headline: Stocks drop post-Wednesday's gains on Trump tariffs; Dollar falls. Inflation reports expected. Tesla, Nvidia, Palantir down.

news.headline: Rithm Capital Corp. will release Q1 2025 financial results on April 25, 2025. Conference call at 8:00 A.M. Eastern Time.

news.summary: In Boise, Idaho, on April 10, 2025, PCS Edventures!, Inc. revealed a significant development. The company, known for its K-12 STEM education initiatives, declared a new share repurchase program. The Board of Directors approved the repurchase of up to 10 million shares of its common stock within the next three years. This move is substantial as it accounts for about 8.18% of the total PCSV shares outstanding as of the end of December 31, 2024.
news.headline: PCS Edventures! (PCSV) to repurchase up to 10 million shares (8.18% outstanding) over 3 years. From GLOBE NEWSWIRE on April 10, 2025.

news.summary: In St. Louis on April 10, 2025, Centene Corporation (NYSE: CNC), a prominent healthcare organization dedicated to improving people's health, revealed that its Nevada branch, SilverSummit Healthplan, Inc. (SilverSummit), has been chosen by the Nevada Department.
news.headline: Centene's SilverSummit Healthplan wins Nevada Medicaid contract, announced April 10, 2025. NYSE: CNC.

news.summary: North Carolina representative Neal Jackson introduced the North Carolina Digital Asset Freedom Act, proposing to accept qualifying digital assets as a legally recognized form of payment and for taxes, with specific criteria benefiting assets like Bitcoin. These include a $750 billion minimum market cap, over $10 billion in daily trading volume, 10+ years of market history, censorship resistance, proof-of-work consensus, lack of central authority, high network uptime, and a supply cap. This move is part of state-led Bitcoin strategic reserve legislation in response to inflation concerns and a depreciating currency. Previously, North Carolina legislators overrode former Governor Roy Cooper's veto to ban central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) in a bipartisan vote. Dan Spuller of the Blockchain Association praised this stance against CBDCs, stating it was a missed opportunity when the veto happened.
news.headline: NC lawmaker introduces bill recognizing digital assets as payment and taxes. Criteria include $750B+ market cap, $10B+ daily trading volume. Decentralized assets align with economic principles. State also rejects CBDCs. Magazine covers Bitcoiners, blockchain issues.

news.summary: In San Diego, California on April 10, 2025, HUMBL, Inc. (OTC: HMBL), a U.S. company centered on technology, revealed the mutual termination of its $2 million Share Exchange Agreement and Master Distribution Agreement with NUBURU, Inc. The unwinding of these agreements is effective immediately.
news.headline: HUMBL ends $2 million deal with NUBURU. Mutual unwinding of Share Exchange Agreement and Master Distribution Agreement.

news.summary: In Montreal on April 10, 2025, Mosaic Minerals Corporation (CSE: MOC) disclosed the decision to cease lithium exploration in their Jamésie, Quebec properties. The company, now emphasizing gold (Amanda) and nickel (Gaboury) potential, will shift its focus to these assets. Notably, both Amanda and Gaboury properties are entirely owned by Mosaic.
news.headline: Mosaic drops lithium exploration, acquires Amanda Property, focuses on gold and nickel, all properties 100% owned.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, Targa Resources Corp. (NYSE: TRGP) announced an increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $1.00 per common share, totaling $4.00 annually for the first quarter of 2025. This marks a 33% rise from the previous year's first quarter dividend. The payment date for this dividend is set for May 15, 2025, to all holders of record as of April 30, 2025.
news.headline: Targa Resources Corp. raises quarterly dividend to $1.00 per common share, a 33% increase over last year, payable May 15, 2025.

news.summary: Renowned developers are collaborating to create 26 luxury oceanfront estate residences worldwide, complete with top-tier amenities, carefully selected fixtures, and customization options for interior design packages. Architectural renderings can be accessed at the provided link. The project is spearheaded by Starwood Capital Group, a leading private investment firm based in Miami. The development is set to introduce opulent living spaces to prospective buyers seeking unparalleled quality and design.
news.headline: Starwood Capital Group & Fontainebleau Development introduce 26 luxury oceanfront homes in Jupiter Island, Southeast Florida.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that around 1,500 new cases of COVID-19 have been confirmed in the last 24 hours. This marks a significant increase in the total number of cases in the country, which now stands at over 50,000. Additionally, there have been 25 new fatalities reported, bringing the total number of deaths to 2,500. These numbers highlight the ongoing challenge posed by the pandemic and the importance of adhering to safety guidelines to curb the spread of the virus.
news.headline: Indonesians rich send wealth abroad as Prabowo policies raise concerns.

news.summary: Last month, the unemployment rate in the country dropped to 4.6%, the lowest it has been in the past two years. The economy also added 250,000 new jobs during the same period. Experts credit this growth to increased consumer spending and business investments. Additionally, the stock market saw a 5% increase, reaching a record high of 35,000 points. This positive economic outlook is expected to continue in the coming months, with forecasts predicting further job growth and stability in the market.
news.headline: Sharp swings in stocks due to Trump's tariff policies highlight risks. (Characters: 69)

news.summary: In recent reports, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.8%, which is the lowest it has been in the past ten years. This decrease is significant as it has led to a total of 500,000 new jobs being created in the last quarter alone. Additionally, the stock market has seen a significant increase, with the main index rising by 10% in the last month. This positive economic growth is attributed to various factors, including increased consumer spending and a rise in exports. Experts are optimistic about the future outlook of the economy based on these recent developments.
news.headline: Conflict between songwriters and Spotify affects podcasters.

news.summary: Last week, it was reported that the stock market experienced a significant drop, with the S&P 500 index losing 3.5% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling by 800 points. This decline was attributed to concerns over rising inflation and interest rates. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite also saw a decrease of 2.5%. Investors were particularly worried about the impact of higher borrowing costs on companies' profitability. The energy sector was hit hard, with oil prices decreasing by 4%. Overall, the market volatility reflected a cautious sentiment among traders, as they await further economic data and Federal Reserve announcements.
news.headline: Trump's tariff pause celebration premature as tariffs on $200B of Chinese goods remain
collins: central bank credibility critical for maintaining stable prices

news.summary: In Alexandria, Virginia, union leaders and members of UNITE HERE showed support for federal workers during the Emergency Committee on Federal Workforce & Funding Reductions. They presented a plan to address the upcoming budget crisis triggered by extremist actions in DC. Virginia, with 145,000 federal employees and over $100 billion in federal contracts projected for 2023, faces significant losses due to the harmful policies of President Trump and Elon Musk's DOGE team. The united effort aims to mitigate the impact on the workforce and economy.
news.headline: Union leaders and UNITE HERE members in NoVA support Federal Workers, urge lawmakers to raise gaming tax rates to address budget shortfalls.

news.summary: Miter Brands based in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, revealed that Diamond Glass has received the prestigious title of 2025 Sustainable Product of the Year from Green Builder magazine.
news.headline: MITER Brands' Diamond Glass named one of Green Builder magazine's 2025 Sustainable Products of the Year.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global unemployment rate has reached 6.5% in light of the ongoing economic challenges, resulting in approximately 213 million people without jobs. The report also stated that youth unemployment remains a pressing issue, with nearly 71 million individuals between the ages of 15-24 years old facing joblessness. The effects of the COVID-19 pandemic have further exacerbated this situation, leading to a greater need for sustainable employment opportunities worldwide. The report emphasizes the essential role of governments and businesses in implementing effective strategies to address this growing concern and support job creation initiatives for a more stable economic future.
news.headline: Chemtrade releases info on North Vancouver chlor-alkali plant; $30 million investment for upgrades.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate dropped to 4.5% in the last quarter, a significant decrease from the previous 6.2% rate. Additionally, the economy added 250,000 jobs, surpassing expectations. The construction sector saw the most growth, with a 3.8% increase in employment. Furthermore, wages increased by 2.3% on average, indicating positive economic growth. Experts predict that the trend will continue in the upcoming months, leading to a more stable job market.
news.headline: Wealthy Indonesians concerned with Prabowo's policies are sending their assets abroad..

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: CRNX) disclosed that the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors had authorized the issuance of non-qualified stock options for the acquisition of 124,950 shares of its common stock. Additionally, they approved the granting of 84,725 restricted stock unit ("RSU") awards to 26 new non-executive staff. These awards were provided under the Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc. 2021 Employment Inducement Incentive Award Plan (the “2021 Inducement Plan”) as essential inducements for these employees joining the company, as per Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).
news.headline: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals granted 124,950 stock options and 84,725 RSU awards to 26 new employees on April 10, 2025.

news.summary: Dublin-based Allegion plc (NYSE: ALLE), a prominent global security products and solutions company, has revealed a quarterly dividend of $0.51 per ordinary share. This dividend will be paid out on June 30, 2025, to shareholders of record as of June 13, 2025. news.summary: Bayer AG's Vitrakvi (larotrectinib) has received full approval from the U.S. Food And Drug Administration (FDA) for adult and pediatric patients with solid tumors containing the neurotrophic receptor tyrosine kinase (NTRK) gene fusion. The drug has shown significant and lasting responses in a range of NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors. Initially granted accelerated approval in November 2018, Vitrakvi's full approval marks a milestone in its efficacy for patients. For more details, refer to Benzinga.com.
news.headline: FDA grants full approval to Bayer's Vitrakvi for NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors. Previously given accelerated approval in 2018.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.7%, the lowest rate in the past decade. This signifies a significant decrease from the previous rate of 4.2%. The report also indicated that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in the past month, surpassing expectations. These numbers point towards a strong and growing job market, with various sectors experiencing growth and contributing to the overall positive trend in employment. This decrease in unemployment and increase in job creation are positive indicators for the country's economic health and stability.
